content_of_post_to_answer: What makes a person-centred approach important in mental health care?
answer: A person-centred approach matters because mental health care is not only about treating symptoms; it is about understanding the person living with those symptoms. Two people with the same diagnosis may need very different kinds of support because their histories, values, fears and strengths differ. The overlooked truth is that feeling seen is not a soft extra in care—it often becomes the foundation that makes meaningful change possible.
